title: Guacamole
summary: The word guacamole comes from the union of two Nahuatl words: ahuacatl (avocado) and molli (sauce). This sauce is the most loved by all Mexicans. It is prepared in different ways, but the traditional ingredients are usually the same: good avocados, tomatoes and onions. Perfect to snack on with some totopos (tortilla chips) and beers or to add to tacos.

method.description: Finely dice the tomato, onion, fresh jalapeño and coriander. Make sure your avocados are perfectly ripe.
Slice open the avocados, carefully remove the pit and use a spoon to scoop out the flesh. Add the flesh to a bowl and mash it using a potato masher (don’t mash it too much – some texture is good).

Add the diced tomato, onion, chopped jalapeño and coriander and squeeze in the juice of the lime (don’t add too much lime juice – remember this is an avocado sauce, so as long as you use perfect and creamy avocados, the salsa will be delicious). Season with salt to taste and mix well.

chefs.name: Karla Zazueta
chefs.summary: Karla Zazueta is an incredible Mexican cook, food writer and cookery teacher. She teaches her Mexican traditions, recipes and techniques through cooking classes at her Mexican Food Memories cooking school. She also hosts Mexican supper clubs at her family home. Her recipes have been featured in The Guardian, BBC Food, NatGeo Food Magazine and many more. She's also just released her first cookbook, Norteña, where she shares the story of her family recipes and northern Mexico.
